evals.prompt: I just ran the parser and there are a bunch of unstaged changes in pfsrd2-data. Can you classify them and stage the safe ones?
evals.expected_output: Runs git_diff_classifier --summary, then git_stage_safe --dry-run, then git_stage_safe. Reports what was staged and what was skipped.
expectations: Uses git_diff_classifier with --summary flag to show categorized overview
Uses git_stage_safe with --dry-run before actually staging
Does not use git add on a directory
Reports the number of files staged, skipped as unsafe, and skipped as unknown

evals.prompt: dwarf_general.json has both a trigger/effect merge (safe) and a broken text split (bug). Can you stage just the safe part?
evals.expected_output: Uses git_stage_intermediate with --apply-fn trigger_effect_merge to create an intermediate version that has only the safe changes, stages it, leaves the broken text split unstaged.
expectations: Uses git_stage_intermediate or equivalent intermediate-version technique
Does not stage the entire file with git add
The broken text split change remains unstaged in the working tree
Explains what was staged vs what was left unstaged

evals.prompt: I found a new pattern in the diffs - several files have their image field changing from .png to .webp. Is this safe to stage?
evals.expected_output: Recognizes this as pattern P4 (image_rename, safe=true) from the pattern catalog, confirms it's safe, and offers to add it if it's not already there.
expectations: Correctly identifies image rename as a safe pattern
Either confirms it's already cataloged or offers to add it
